content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as recursive self-generalizing agents, where the memory store itself acts as a secondary curiosity-driven agent that prioritizes which unlabeled new experiences to encode for the primary task agent. This creates a two-agent active learning loop where the memory agent independently pursues information gain to improve the primary agent’s downstream performance, rather than having the primary agent select memory contents alone.
